purpose: Map real Top-K implementations to contracts, algorithms, baseline roles, and custom-kernel attack surfaces.
evidence_note: No GPU measurements or rocprofiler/rocprof counters were added with this survey. Treat all implementation notes as source-inspection guidance until benchmark records are attached.
must_not_claim: A result against one stack is a result against all Top-K contracts.
Unsorted hipCUB or FlashInfer output is equivalent to sorted deterministic output without a timed repair.
Sampling Top-K, masking Top-K, MoE routing Top-K, and values/indices Top-K are interchangeable.
vLLM has one Top-K implementation independent of runtime dispatch.
Workspace, row-state memset, framework allocation, or contiguous-copy costs can be ignored in fair timing.

implementations.default_sortedness: sorted=True at Python API; sorted=False available
implementations.determinism_and_ties: PyTorch docs do not guarantee stable indices for tied elements.
algorithm_notes: ROCm path gathers Top-K with radix kth-value selection, then sorts only selected results when sorted output is requested.
Single-block path uses radixSelect plus gather of values better than or equal to the kth boundary.
Multi-block path uses radix digit counts, per-slice scans, kth counts, and a gather pass for large slices.
Input is made contiguous before launch.
workspace_notes: Sorted selected-output fallback can allocate sortedValues and sortedIndices.
Framework dispatch and tensor allocation must be recorded separately.
custom_attack_surfaces: direct contiguous layout without framework copy
unsorted membership when downstream accepts it
deterministic tie policy with pair comparisons
implementations.baseline_role: Framework oracle and practical baseline; not necessarily strongest serving baseline.

implementations.determinism_and_ties: Requires cuda::execution::determinism::not_guaranteed; tied boundary subsets may vary.
algorithm_notes: AIR radix Top-K with histogram/filter passes.
Candidate buffers are allocated from hipCUB temp storage.
Primitive keys are radix-twiddled; custom decomposed keys use field radix traits.
Tests validate unordered membership or sort outputs before comparison.
workspace_notes: Record temp-storage query, temporary bytes, and allocation or reuse policy.
Candidate buffers include ping-pong key buffers and optional indices.
implementations.baseline_role: Strong unsorted membership baseline; contract mismatch for sorted deterministic Top-K without repair.

implementations.default_sortedness: path-dependent; sampling paths may not expose sorted candidates
implementations.determinism_and_ties: Docs do not guarantee particular tied-probability treatment; direct kernels have path-specific behavior.
algorithm_notes: Top-K sampling is a two-stage hipCUB BlockReduce pipeline with temp ids/values and rocRAND/CURAND sampling.
Top-K sampling supports 1 <= k <= 1024 in the inspected path.
topkLastDim includes AIR/radix Top-K and a small inputLength <= 128, k <= 8 MoE-style reduce path.
Classic Top-P uses hipCUB segmented radix sort; AIR Top-P uses histogram/radix thresholding.
workspace_notes: Top-K sampling workspace includes full temp logits plus temp Top-K ids and values.
Record rocRAND/CURAND state and whether RNG is timed.
Record sampler backend: Torch Sampler, TRTLLM Sampler, or direct kernel.
custom_attack_surfaces: no finished-state or heterogeneous request handling
implementations.baseline_role: Serving baseline and edge-case reference; fair only when the same serving boundary and features are timed.

implementations.default_sortedness: flashinfer.top_k defaults to unsorted output; sorted=True is explicit
implementations.determinism_and_ties: deterministic=False is default for speed; tie-break modes force deterministic mode.
algorithm_notes: Radix Top-K path with cached 1 MB row-state buffer.
Cluster exact path is selected on gfx target major 10 when nondeterministic and graph constraints allow it.
FilteredTopK requires 128 KB dynamic shared memory.
Deterministic sorted output is handled on device for k <= 2048; other sorted cases may use torch.sort.
Sampling APIs implement rejection-style GPU sampling without explicit sorting.
workspace_notes: Record row-state cache, overflow buffers, dynamic shared memory, graph-safe path, seed, offset, and sorted fallback.
Include softmax timing when using probability-consuming APIs unless the custom path also starts from probabilities.
custom_attack_surfaces: avoid Python dispatch and row-state cache
architecture-specific fallback for limited shared memory
implementations.baseline_role: Primary inference Top-K and sampling baseline, especially for large vocabularies.

implementations.default_sortedness: runtime path dependent; Triton path masks logits rather than returning sorted Top-K
implementations.determinism_and_ties: path dependent across FlashInfer, Triton, PyTorch, and custom HIP paths.
algorithm_notes: Sampler dispatch can choose FlashInfer, Triton, PyTorch-native, ROCm AITER, XPU, or custom HIP paths.
FlashInfer is skipped when processed logits/logprobs or per-request generators are required.
Triton path is Qrita-inspired pivot masking with duplicate-boundary handling and Top-P after Top-K.
persistent_topk is a sparse-attention ragged indexer for FP32 and k in {512, 1024, 2048}.
workspace_notes: Record selected path, environment flags, logits.contiguous copy, cached buffers, row-state cudaMemsetAsync, and output mode.
Do not time against the label vLLM without knowing which backend ran.
implementations.baseline_role: Serving baseline whose actual path must be observed and logged.

implementations.default_sortedness: framework API exposes sorted flag; inspected ROCm path uses sorted index production
implementations.determinism_and_ties: path dependent; inspect pair compare behavior for exact tie policy.
algorithm_notes: For k > 30 or instance_num == 1, OneFlow uses a hipCUB-based full sort path and copies first k indices.
For k <= 30 and multiple instances, each thread maintains a heap over a strided subset, then bitonic-sorts shared entries.
workspace_notes: Full-sort path allocates sorted input, indices, sorted indices, and hipCUB temp storage.
Heap path consumes shared memory proportional to num_heap * heap_size * sizeof(Entry).
custom_attack_surfaces: register list instead of heap for tiny k
avoid sorting all heap entries when unsorted output is accepted
retune heap-vs-sort crossover by GPU and shape
implementations.baseline_role: Compact framework reference for heap/list/full-sort crossover.

implementations.default_sortedness: routing map/probability output, not generic sorted Top-K values
implementations.determinism_and_ties: routing-path dependent; exact tie policy must be tested for MoE workloads.
algorithm_notes: One warp handles one token with shared-memory buffers for scores, top-k scores, top-k indices, group scores, and masked scores.
Supports grouped Top-K and score functions before or after Top-K.
Uses naive Top-K for topk < 16 and radix Top-K for larger topk.
workspace_notes: Record shared memory size, group buffers, score function, backward requirements, and routing_map layout.
custom_attack_surfaces: fused score transform and routing writes
implementations.baseline_role: MoE routing reference, not generic row-major vocab Top-K.

implementations.default_sortedness: fused Top-K softmax over epilogue values; not a standalone sorted Top-K API
implementations.determinism_and_ties: example warns repeated top-k values can be problematic because values, not unique value-index pairs, are tracked.
algorithm_notes: CDNA3 epilogue visitor tracks static Top-K values and logsumexp.
K=2 and K=4 are performance optimized by default.
Reduction uses warp shuffles and then masks non-top-k outputs.
workspace_notes: Record CDNA3 requirement, epilogue shared storage carveout, static k, and tile assumptions.
custom_attack_surfaces: move Top-K into GEMM or score epilogue
track value-index pairs for duplicate repair
implementations.baseline_role: Fusion design reference for producer-side Top-K, not generic selection baseline.

custom_kernel_ladder: tiny-k register list or fixed network
